title: RV8GR interrupt enable and IRQ trace
purpose: Package EI, DI, and IRQ release trace behavior so IE, IRQ_FF, PC unchanged, and v1.0 polling-only interrupt behavior are checked together.

chips.role: Dual D flip-flop for IE and IRQ_FF
EI decode source for U31 CLK1

ports.name: PC_FORCE
ports.description: v1.0 has no hardware PC force or interrupt vector path.

wiring.rule: Rising edge occurs for the EI trace only.
IRQ release clocks constant 1 into IRQ_FF.
LOW assertion holds; release HIGH creates the latch edge.

behavior.ei: EI sets IE on the EI_decode rising edge.
behavior.di: DI is inert in v1.0 hardware; it does not clear IE or IRQ_FF.
behavior.irq_low: /IRQ asserted LOW does not latch IRQ_FF.
behavior.irq_release: /IRQ release back HIGH creates a rising edge and latches IRQ_FF.
behavior.sticky: IRQ_FF remains set across later CPU ticks until reset.
behavior.pc: PC is unchanged by EI, DI, /IRQ assertion, and /IRQ release in v1.0.
behavior.polling_only: Software polls IE and IRQ_FF; there is no auto-vector, IRQ acknowledge, or hardware PC force.

timing.clocking: mixed T2 control and external release edge
timing.frequency_boundary: Functional trace proof only; physical edge quality is owned by timing_margins.json.
timing.student_rule: LOW means the IRQ button is pressed or held; the latch happens when the line returns HIGH.

guarantees: DI is inert in v1.0 hardware
/IRQ LOW assertion alone does not latch IRQ_FF
No auto-vector, PC force, IRQ acknowledge, or auto-clear path appears

audience: students ages 10-15, still useful for older learners
